Simon Peter

Paul G. Allen School of Computer Science and Engineering, University of Washington

Office: CSE 540

Paul G. Allen School of Computer Science & Engineering

Box 352350
Seattle, WA 98195
USA

About Me

I am an associate professor in the Paul G. Allen School of Computer Science and Engineering at UW, since January 2022.

I graduated with a Ph.D. (Dr. sc. ETH) in Computer Science at the Swiss Federal Institute of Technology, Zurich, in October 2012. Prior to that I graduated (Dipl.-Inf.) from the Carl-von-Ossietzky University of Oldenburg, Germany in 2006 with a major in Computer Science and a minor in Music. Prior to my position in the Allen School, I was an assistant professor at UT Austin, since 2016.

Students and Postdocs

I have the honor to be advising a number of exceptional graduate students and postdocs:

  • Dhathri Purohith, MSc (Confluent)
  • Kevin Puthusseri, MSc (Google)
  • Amanda Raybuck, Ph.D. (UC Davis)
  • Rajath Shashidhara, Ph.D. (Google)
  • Tim Stamler, Ph.D. (NVIDIA)
  • Theano Stavrinos, Ph.D. (Databricks)
  • Zezhou Wang, MSc

Research

My research interests are in Operating Systems and Networks. My current research projects are in low latency, scalable, and energy-efficient data center system and cloud application design, across the hardware/software continuum. Yes, I work on and with AI, too.

You can find recent and ongoing projects on the web pages of the various centers and groups that I am either leading or am involved with. In particular, FOCI and SyFI.

Teaching

Fall 2025: CSE453/M553 Data Center Systems
Spring 2025: CSE481A Operating Systems Capstone
Spring 2024: CSE481A Operating Systems Capstone
Fall 2023: CSE453/M553 Data Center Systems
Spring 2023: CSE481A Operating Systems Capstone
Fall 2022: CSE453/M553 Data Center Systems
Spring 2022: CSE599 Datacenters

Spring 2021: Multicore Operating Systems Implementation (378)
Fall 2020: Data Centers (395T)

Vita and Publications

Please, read my CV. It has all of the information and I keep it updated. I also have a Google Scholar page, if you prefer a quick overview of my publications, but it typically lags behind my CV.

Personal

Apart from CS, I enjoy making and listening to music. I used to play the violin and compose electronic music on the computer. The advent of reasonably precise DJ controllers recently allow me to resurrect many of the old sounds in new ways. I enjoy going skiing in the winter and hiking in the summer.

I was born in Oldenburg, situated in north-west Germany, and enjoyed living a good deal of my youth in the cozy area of Bad Zwischenahn. In my teenage years, I used to be active in the Demoscene, a computer art subculture and I have fond memories of the good old times.
